name: jar-skills
description: Agent skill stack for manuscripts targeted at the Journal of Accounting Research (JAR), one of the top-three accounting journals, sponsored by the Chookaszian Accounting Research Center at the University of Chicago Booth School of Business and published by Wiley-Blackwell. Built around JAR's empirical-archival capital-markets tradition (the Ball-Brown lineage), its mandatory data-and-code sharing regime, the Ray Ball JAR Annual Conference, and its Registered Reports program. Covers topic selection, institutional/economic theory development, identification-driven research design, large-sample archival analysis, contribution framing, literature positioning, JAR house-style exhibits and prose, the Wiley Research Exchange submission and tiered-fee gate, the double-anonymized review process, and R&R rebuttals. Bilingual en / zh-CN docs.
